protocols.description: A set of strings representing HTTP protocols.

message_countback.description: Number of messages in the chat history to Vectorize/Cache

ignore_assistant_prompts.description: Ignore and discard any assistant prompts when Vectorizing the request

cache_control.description: When enabled, respect the Cache-Control behaviors defined in RFC7234.

exact_caching.description: When enabled, a first check for exact query will be done. It will impact DB size

llm_format.description: LLM input and output format and schema to use

auth_password.description: The password to authenticate with, if the forward proxy is protected by basic authentication.

no_proxy.description: Comma-separated list of hosts that should not be proxied.

http_proxy_host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

http_proxy_port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

https_proxy_host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

https_proxy_port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

proxy_scheme.description: The proxy scheme to use when connecting. Only `http` is supported.

auth_username.description: The username to authenticate with, if the forward proxy is protected by basic authentication.

ignore_system_prompts.description: Ignore and discard any system prompts when Vectorizing the request

ignore_tool_prompts.description: Ignore and discard any tool prompts when Vectorizing the request

stop_on_failure.description: Halt the LLM request process in case of a caching system failure

cache_ttl.description: TTL in seconds of cache entities. Must be a value greater than 0.

param_name.description: If AI model requires authentication via query parameter, specify its name here.

azure_client_id.description: If azure_use_managed_identity is set to true, and you need to use a different user-assigned identity for this LLM instance, set the client ID.

gcp_use_service_account.description: Use service account auth for GCP-based providers and models.

gcp_oauth_token_url.description: Custom OAuth token URL for GCP authentication. Useful for restricted network environments or custom GCP endpoints. If null, Kong will use the default Google OAuth token endpoint.

aws_secret_access_key.description: Set this if you are using an AWS provider (Bedrock) and you are authenticating using static IAM User credentials. Setting this will override the AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY environment variable for this plugin instance.

allow_override.description: If enabled, the authorization header or parameter can be overridden in the request by the value configured in the plugin.

header_value.description: Specify the full auth header value for 'header_name', for example 'Bearer key' or just 'key'.

azure_use_managed_identity.description: Set true to use the Azure Cloud Managed Identity (or user-assigned identity) to authenticate with Azure-provider models.

azure_tenant_id.description: If azure_use_managed_identity is set to true, and you need to use a different user-assigned identity for this LLM instance, set the tenant ID.

aws_access_key_id.description: Set this if you are using an AWS provider (Bedrock) and you are authenticating using static IAM User credentials. Setting this will override the AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID environment variable for this plugin instance.

header_name.description: If AI model requires authentication via Authorization or API key header, specify its name here.

gcp_service_account_json.description: Set this field to the full JSON of the GCP service account to authenticate, if required. If null (and gcp_use_service_account is true), Kong will attempt to read from environment variable `GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT`.

gcp_metadata_url.description: Custom metadata URL for GCP authentication. Useful for restricted network environments or custom GCP endpoints. If null, Kong will use the default Google metadata endpoint.

param_value.description: Specify the full parameter value for 'param_name'.

param_location.description: Specify whether the 'param_name' and 'param_value' options go in a query string, or the POST form/JSON body.

azure_client_secret.description: If azure_use_managed_identity is set to true, and you need to use a different user-assigned identity for this LLM instance, set the client secret.

api_endpoint.description: If running Gemini on Vertex, specify the regional API endpoint (hostname only).

project_id.description: If running Gemini on Vertex, specify the project ID.

location_id.description: If running Gemini on Vertex, specify the location ID.

use_cache.description: Use the cache layer on the inference API

wait_for_model.description: Wait for the model if it is not ready

workspace_instance_id.description: Workspace Instance ID ('dbc-xxx-yyy') for Databricks model serving.

instance.description: Instance name for Azure OpenAI hosted models.

deployment_id.description: Deployment ID for Azure OpenAI instances.

video_output_s3_uri.description: S3 URI (s3://bucket/prefix) where Bedrock will store generated video files. Required for video generation.

batch_bucket_prefix.description: S3 URI prefix (s3://bucket/prefix/) where Bedrock will get input files from and store results to for native batch API.

embeddings_normalize.description: If using AWS providers (Bedrock), set to true to normalize the embeddings.

batch_role_arn.description: AWS role arn used for calling batch API. Try to get the value from request if ommited.

aws_region.description: If using AWS providers (Bedrock) you can override the `AWS_REGION` environment variable by setting this option.

aws_assume_role_arn.description: If using AWS providers (Bedrock) you can assume a different role after authentication with the current IAM context is successful.

aws_role_session_name.description: If using AWS providers (Bedrock), set the identifier of the assumed role session.

aws_sts_endpoint_url.description: If using AWS providers (Bedrock), override the STS endpoint URL when assuming a different role.

performance_config_latency.description: Force the client's performance configuration 'latency' for all requests. Leave empty to let the consumer select the performance configuration.

provider.description: AI provider format to use for embeddings API

strategy.description: which vector database driver to use

dimensions.description: the desired dimensionality for the vectors

threshold.description: the default similarity threshold for accepting semantic search results (float). Higher threshold means more results are considered similar.

distance_metric.description: the distance metric to use for vector searches

port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

read_timeout.description: An integer representing a timeout in milliseconds. Must be between 0 and 2^31-2.

password.description: Password to use for Redis connections. If undefined, no AUTH commands are sent to Redis.

ssl_verify.description: If set to true, verifies the validity of the server SSL certificate. If setting this parameter, also configure `lua_ssl_trusted_certificate` in `kong.conf` to specify the CA (or server) certificate used by your Redis server. You may also need to configure `lua_ssl_verify_depth` accordingly.

connection_is_proxied.description: If the connection to Redis is proxied (e.g. Envoy), set it `true`. Set the `host` and `port` to point to the proxy address.

connect_timeout.description: An integer representing a timeout in milliseconds. Must be between 0 and 2^31-2.

sentinel_username.description: Sentinel username to authenticate with a Redis Sentinel instance. If undefined, ACL authentication won't be performed. This requires Redis v6.2.0+.

keepalive_pool_size.description: The size limit for every cosocket connection pool associated with every remote server, per worker process. If neither `keepalive_pool_size` nor `keepalive_backlog` is specified, no pool is created. If `keepalive_pool_size` isn't specified but `keepalive_backlog` is specified, then the pool uses the default value. Try to increase (e.g. 512) this value if latency is high or throughput is low.

sentinel_master.description: Sentinel master to use for Redis connections. Defining this value implies using Redis Sentinel.

sentinel_role.description: Sentinel role to use for Redis connections when the `redis` strategy is defined. Defining this value implies using Redis Sentinel.

host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

sentinel_nodes.description: Sentinel node addresses to use for Redis connections when the `redis` strategy is defined. Defining this field implies using a Redis Sentinel. The minimum length of the array is 1 element.

host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

send_timeout.description: An integer representing a timeout in milliseconds. Must be between 0 and 2^31-2.

sentinel_password.description: Sentinel password to authenticate with a Redis Sentinel instance. If undefined, no AUTH commands are sent to Redis Sentinels.

auth_provider.description: Auth providers to be used to authenticate to a Cloud Provider's Redis instance.

aws_cache_name.description: The name of the AWS Elasticache cluster when `auth_provider` is set to `aws`.

aws_region.description: The region of the AWS ElastiCache cluster when `auth_provider` is set to `aws`.

aws_is_serverless.description: This flag specifies whether the cluster is serverless when auth_provider is set to `aws`.

aws_secret_access_key.description: AWS Secret Access Key to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `aws`.

aws_assume_role_arn.description: The ARN of the IAM role to assume for generating ElastiCache IAM authentication tokens.

aws_role_session_name.description: The session name for the temporary credentials when assuming the IAM role.

gcp_service_account_json.description: GCP Service Account JSON to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `gcp`.

aws_access_key_id.description: AWS Access Key ID to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `aws`.

azure_client_id.description: Azure Client ID to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `azure`.

azure_client_secret.description: Azure Client Secret to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `azure`.

azure_tenant_id.description: Azure Tenant ID to be used for authentication when `auth_provider` is set to `azure`.

cloud_authentication.description: Cloud auth related configs for connecting to a Cloud Provider's Redis instance.

database.description: Database to use for the Redis connection when using the `redis` strategy

ssl.description: If set to true, uses SSL to connect to Redis.

username.description: Username to use for Redis connections. If undefined, ACL authentication won't be performed. This requires Redis v6.0.0+. To be compatible with Redis v5.x.y, you can set it to `default`.

keepalive_backlog.description: Limits the total number of opened connections for a pool. If the connection pool is full, connection queues above the limit go into the backlog queue. If the backlog queue is full, subsequent connect operations fail and return `nil`. Queued operations (subject to set timeouts) resume once the number of connections in the pool is less than `keepalive_pool_size`. If latency is high or throughput is low, try increasing this value. Empirically, this value is larger than `keepalive_pool_size`.

ip.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

cluster_nodes.description: Cluster addresses to use for Redis connections when the `redis` strategy is defined. Defining this field implies using a Redis Cluster. The minimum length of the array is 1 element.

server_name.description: A string representing an SNI (server name indication) value for TLS.

host.description: the host of the pgvector database

port.description: the port of the pgvector database

user.description: the user of the pgvector database

password.description: the password of the pgvector database

database.description: the database of the pgvector database

timeout.description: the timeout of the pgvector database

ssl.description: whether to use ssl for the pgvector database

ssl_verify.description: whether to verify ssl for the pgvector database

ssl_required.description: whether ssl is required for the pgvector database

ssl_version.description: the ssl version to use for the pgvector database

ssl_cert.description: the path of ssl cert to use for the pgvector database

ssl_cert_key.description: the path of ssl cert key to use for the pgvector database

consumer.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests where the specified has been authenticated. (Note that some plugins can not be restricted to consumers this way.). Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the authenticated Consumer.

consumer_group.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests where the specified consumer group has been authenticated. (Note that some plugins can not be restricted to consumers groups this way.). Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the authenticated Consumer Groups

route.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via the specified route. Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the route being used.

service.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via one of the routes belonging to the specified Service. Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the Service being matched.
